sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me reconsider the problem. I've tried matching all three source panels and various combinations without success. Let me look at the center pieces more carefully, since they seem to be different across panels.
Looking at the segmentation data:
- Top-left center: has gray (g), white (W), and red (R) components
- Top-right center: has gray (g), white (W), and red (R) components
- Bottom-left center: has white (W), gray (g), and red (R) components
- Bottom-right center: has white (W), gray (g), and red (R) components
The center pieces have different hash values, indicating different patterns. Maybe the center piece in the bottom-right panel indicates which source panel to match?
Let me compare the center piece hashes:
- Top-left center components: g(34f0754d), W(81032741), g(156e67f8), W(81032741), R(700b1733), W(81032741), g(34f0754d), W(94540938)
- Top-right center components: g(34f0754d), W(81032741), g(34f0754d), W(81032741), R(700b1733), W(81032741), g(34f0754d), W(81032741)
- Bottom-left center: W(4cdfa21f), g(c00d5814), ...
- Bottom-right center: W(...), g(...), ...
Actually, let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to make the bottom-right panel match a specific pattern that I haven't tried yet. Let me try all red.
Current bottom-right: b, R, b / R, center, R / b, R, b
To get all red: R, R, R / R, center, R / R, R, R
Need: (0,0) b->R, (0,2) b->R, (2,0) b->R, (2,2) b->R
That's 4 clicks. Let me try this.
